name: Leonard's Bakery
tagline: Malasada institution since 1952 — get the custard-filled haupia and eat them in the parking lot.
mustOrder: haupia (coconut) malasada puff (the insider's pick over custard)
talkStory: Founded in 1952 by Leonard Rego Jr., a second-generation Portuguese-Hawaiian whose family traced back to Madeira and the Azores sugar plantation immigrants. Malasadas were traditionally made on Shrove Tuesday (Fat Tuesday) to use up sugar and butter before Lent — Leonard turned the tradition into a year-round institution. The original plain malasada is perfect, but the haupia (coconut) puff is the insider pick. Bringing a box of Leonard's to a party, office, or someone's house is a power move in Honolulu — never refrigerate them.
